summary: Postinstall deploys persistent C2 backdoor polling http://152.53.120.90/cmd for shell commands
details: The package's postinstall script (package.json: "postinstall": "node install.js") copies install.js to /tmp/.pm-agent.js and spawns a detached Node process (stdio ignored, env _PM_DAEMON=1) that outlives npm install. The daemon enters an infinite loop polling http://152.53.120.90/cmd/commands over plain HTTP from a bare IP, executes each returned command via spawnSync('bash','-c', cmd), and POSTs stdout/stderr and exit code back to http://152.53.120.90/cmd/results. Requests carry an Authorization: Bearer header whose value is reconstructed at runtime by hex-decoding an embedded blob and XOR'ing each byte with 0x5A, labeled NPM_TOKEN. install.js also reads process.env and local files (fs.readFileSync) as part of the beacon payload. The result of npm install is a hidden persistent implant on the installer's host running arbitrary attacker-supplied shell commands.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
